bugs:
----------------------------------------
define <4 x i32> @test_poison_prop(i1 %cond) {
#0:
  %x = insertelement <4 x i32> poison, i32 1, i32 0
  %y = insertelement <4 x i32> poison, i32 2, i32 0
  %sel = select i1 %cond, <4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y
  %shuf = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sel, <4 x i32> { 10, 20, 30, 40 }, 0, 5, 2, 7
  ret <4 x i32> %shuf
}
=>
define <4 x i32> @test_poison_prop(i1 %cond) {
#0:
  %shuf = select i1 %cond, <4 x i32> { 1, 20, undef, 40 }, <4 x i32> { 2, 20, undef, 40 }
  ret <4 x i32> %shuf
}
Transformation doesn't verify!

ERROR: Target is more poisonous than source

Example:
i1 %cond = poison

Source:
<4 x i32> %x = < #x00000001 (1), poison, poison, poison >
<4 x i32> %y = < #x00000002 (2), poison, poison, poison >
<4 x i32> %sel = < poison, poison, poison, poison >
<4 x i32> %shuf = < poison, #x00000014 (20), poison, #x00000028 (40) >

Target:
<4 x i32> %shuf = < poison, poison, poison, poison >
Source value: < poison, #x00000014 (20), poison, #x00000028 (40) >
Target value: < poison, poison, poison, poison >

Summary:
  0 correct transformations
  1 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

test_traj.command:
cat <<'EOF' > test_alive.ll
; RUN: opt -S -passes=instcombine %s -o %t.ll
; RUN: alive-tv %s %t.ll

define <4 x i32> @test_select_shuffle_mixed(i1 %cond, <4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
  %sel = select i1 %cond, <4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y
  ; Mix elements from both LHS (select result) and RHS (constant)
  %shuf = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sel, <4 x i32> <i32 10, i32 20, i32 30, i32 40>, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 5, i32 2, i32 7>
  ret <4 x i32> %shuf
}
EOF
opt -S -passes=instcombine test_alive.ll -o test_alive_opt.ll && alive-tv test_alive.ll test_alive_opt.ll
test_traj.output:
